reasoning: Hess Corporation (HES) was acquired by Chevron and delisted from the NYSE on July 18, 2025. The resolution criteria explicitly state: "Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price." This hardcodes the resolution value to the stock's final trading price (which appears to be 148.970) for all dates after the delisting. Accordingly, the market close price for the base date (2026-04-12) resolves to the final close price, and the price on each subsequent resolution date also resolves to the exact same final close price. Since the question asks if the price will be strictly "higher" than the price on 2026-04-12, and the value will remain exactly the same, the condition for resolving YES (Price > Price) cannot be met. Probabilities are therefore set at the minimum allowed limit (0.05) for the near-term, and slightly higher for distant dates to strictly comply with the rule that uncertainty must increase over long time horizons (to account for minor edge cases such as platform rule changes, relisting under the exact same ticker, or ambiguous resolution).
